decile_monotonicity.interpretation: there is broad monotonic decline from top decile to bottom decile, so the score is not random; however the slope is shallow

top_bucket_readout.interpretation: the top 10 names beat the bottom 10, but the next 10 names outperform the top 10; this points to weak edge concentration and noisy cutoff behavior at the extreme head of the ranking

conclusion: The calibrated momentum-only score still has a real but weak positive signal, as shown by positive IC and decile monotonicity.
The edge is not concentrated cleanly in the top 10 names; rank 11-20 performs better on average than rank 1-10.

This supports the view that the strategy is failing because the gross edge is thin and the current TopK mapping is extracting a noisy extreme slice of the score rather than a robust high-conviction bucket.
Future candidate design should prefer one-dimensional tests that change score-to-portfolio mapping or top-of-book selection geometry, not more liquidity-guard tweaks.
